description:
Define the canonical post-expansion fragments that compiler2 accepts for scope-shaping definitions and make `Fz.Compiler.define(source, __ENV__)` the sole publication boundary for them. The compiler callback should own one honest publication contract for function/module/protocol/impl fragments and stop depending on a parallel direct-publication path for the same semantic act.

This ticket should: lock down the canonical quoted fragment shapes, make compiler-service application the explicit definition boundary, and remove source-publication assumptions that those same definition families may bypass that boundary.

Signal: equivalent canonical fragments publish identical facts regardless of origin, and compiler2 has one real definition callback for these forms.
title: Compiler2 definition callback: define canonical compiler fragments and narrow publication to Fz.Compiler.define
